preamble: The near-open front unrounded vowel [æ] shifts toward the close front rounded vowel [y] through vowel rounding assimilation before labialized or back consonants. This process occurs allophonically in Athabaskan languages (Babine, Deg Xinag), where vowels acquire rounding as a coarticulation effect preceding labialized velars and uvulars. Rounded vowels and labialized consonants phonetically assimilate through mutual coarticulation.

examples.note: Schwa rounds allophonically before back labialized consonants through rounding assimilation
Rounding assimilation precedes pre-coda consonant retraction from velar to uvular
Rounding assimilation documented in similar contexts to Babine; variable across dialects
Labialized consonants round preceding vowels through coarticulation; documented in Athabaskan, Caucasian, Semitic languages

related.label: Schwa rounding to back vowel in labialized contexts
